scroll-cat-story:
Adding Character To Reading

A tiny character can make scrolling feel less mechanical.

Scroll-Linked Animation

Map document progress to rope length, then trigger a reward interaction at the bottom.

Bottom-Reached Reward

When users finish an article, trigger one short celebratory jump and message bubble.

Design Constraint

Do not block reading flow. Delight should be optional and lightweight.
markdown-pipeline:
Content Should Be Portable

Markdown gives you longevity. Keep metadata in frontmatter and body in plain text.

Build Step Responsibilities

The build step should do exactly three things:

1. Parse frontmatter and markdown.

2. Generate sorted metadata for listing pages.

3. Generate per-slug HTML for detail pages.

Tooling Notes

A tiny Node script is enough when your site is small.
first-signal:
Why Intentional Motion Matters

Motion should answer a user question. If the animation does not teach, guide, or delight with purpose, it is decoration.

Build For Performance First

Start with a 60fps budget. Every effect must justify CPU and memory cost.

Practical Rules

  • Prefer transform and opacity over layout thrashing.
  • Guard high-cost animation by media queries and reduced-motion.
  • Reuse objects when rendering particles.

Closing Thought

Great interfaces feel alive, but never loud.
